mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals strong labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of PAYEMS growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.5% indicates a tightening monetary cycle that raises discount rates on equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high-FEDFUNDS regimes exceed 1.5% in > 40% of cases or Rank IC remains above 0.05 in tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signal stagflationary pressures that compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in co-occurring CPIAUCSL/FEDFUNDS spikes are positive in > 50% of cases or interaction effect size drops below 0.15 in rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction (HOUST) indicates strong demand for housing, which drives corporate sector growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in HOUST growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10) or Rank IC falls below 0.03 in expansionary cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust supply-side capacity utilization that lowers input costs and boosts corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in INDPRO growth periods show no significant outperformance (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction with GDP growth becomes non-significant.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates below 3.5% signal a tight labor market that drives wage inflation and central bank rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in low-UNRATE regimes exceed 1.2% in > 35% of cases or Rank IC remains above 0.04 in tight labor markets.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and GDP (GDPC1) indicates a strong economic expansion that drives equity market optimism and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in co-occurring RSAFS/GDPC1 growth periods are negative in > 45% of cases or interaction effect size drops below 0.20 in rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A declining CPIAUCSL combined with rising FEDFUNDS signals disinflationary pressures that reduce central bank rate hike expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in disinflationary regimes are negative in > 50% of cases or interaction effect size drops below 0.15 in rolling 3-year windows.
